summary: Frontend-only regression executed for merged Instagram timeline viewer using embedded 3 JSON files. Desktop functional checks pass for merge, chronological order, side split, date separators, and time labels; mobile load and sidebar open pass, but mobile sidebar selection/close could not be reliably automated due duplicated test ids/hidden duplicates.

ui_bugs.issue: Duplicate data-testid values exist simultaneously (e.g., sidebar-main-heading / conversation-item-*), causing strict-mode selector ambiguity and flaky automation on mobile sidebar interactions.

integration_issues.flow: Mobile sidebar select conversation -> close overlay
integration_issues.issue: Could not complete reliably in automation because locator resolves to hidden duplicates when overlay is open; needs unique or overlay-scoped test ids for deterministic interaction coverage.

issues: Environment floating badge overlaps chat footer area visually on smaller viewport regions.

action_items: Make sidebar-related data-testid values unique per layout state (or remove hidden duplicate instances from DOM) to support stable mobile interaction testing.
Add explicit close control for mobile sidebar overlay (with dedicated data-testid) to improve UX and testability.
Re-verify mobile sidebar select+close flow after test-id hardening.
critical_code_review_comments: toTimestamp() falls back to Date.now() for invalid timestamps, which can silently reorder malformed messages; consider deterministic fallback or validation error reporting.
mergeMessageFiles() deduplication only compares adjacent sorted messages; non-adjacent duplicates would remain.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Desktop validated: 11 merged messages rendered from 3 embedded files, chronological sequence correct, Aditya right/Chikki left alignment correct, exactly 2 day separators detected, and time labels visible. Mobile validated: app loads, chat rows visible, sidebar overlay opens. Mobile select/close flow needs recheck after resolving duplicate test-id ambiguity.
rca of the issue: RCA (mobile automation instability): App always renders desktop sidebar in DOM (hidden via CSS) and conditionally renders mobile overlay sidebar, so identical data-testid values coexist. Automation strict mode and generic locators resolve to hidden/duplicate targets, preventing deterministic interaction with mobile conversation button. Mitigation: scope test ids under overlay container or generate layout-specific ids; optionally unmount hidden desktop sidebar on mobile.
